attributes.name: biomaterial_provider
attributes.value: Dr. Renee Arias - National Peanut Research Laboratory

biosample.biomaterial_provider: Dr. Renee Arias - National Peanut Research Laboratory

description.comment: Aflatoxins, primarily produced by two phytopathogenic fungi, Aspergillus flavus and Aspergillus parasiticus, are among the most powerful carcinogens. The aflatoxin biosynthesis gene cluster contains 25 genes that are involved in aflatoxin production. Due to the differences in toxin-production among Aspergillus species and its correspondence with their genomics, election of the target gene has to be carefully studied to ensure RNA interference silencing success and efficacy.
description.title: Microbe sample from Aspergillus flavus 79-2

description.title: Aspergillus parvisclerotigenus CBS 121.62 Standard Draft

assembly_info.comments:
URL -- http://genome.jgi.doe.gov/Asppari1
JGI Project ID: 1052297
The DNA was provided by Mikael Andersen (mr@bio.dtu.dk)
The strain is available from CBS-KNAW Collections - Westerdijk Fungal Biodiversity Institute
Contacts: Mikael Andersen (mr@bio.dtu.dk)
Assembly and annotation done by JGI.
The JGI and collaborators endorse the principles for the distribution and use of large scale sequencing data adopted by the larger genome sequencing community and urge users of this data to follow them. It is our intention to publish the work of this project in a timely fashion and we welcome collaborative interaction on the project and analysis.
(http://www.genome.gov/page.cfm?pageID=10506376)
